src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/Web.why_exists: Compilation error: the attribute 'ignoredRequestPatterns' no longer exists on the @EnableCookieFilter annotation in the upgraded library version.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/Web.general_idea: Add required toolKeys attribute to @EnableLtiClient annotation to comply with updated library API.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/Web.why_exists: Compilation error: the @EnableLtiClient annotation now requires the 'toolKeys' attribute, or the previous default behavior is no longer supported/valid in the upgraded library.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Remove unused imports that are no longer referenced in the class body.
Remove commented-out annotation declarations that are no longer needed.
Remove large block of commented-out bean definitions for DataSource, EntityManagerFactory, and TransactionManager.
Add @EnableWebSecurity annotation to the main configuration class to ensure Spring Security is properly activated in the new version.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: Spring Security 5.7+ (common in Spring Boot 2.7+/3.x) requires explicit @EnableWebSecurity or the use of SecurityFilterChain beans; relying on auto-configuration without it may lead to missing security filters or deprecation warnings that break strict builds.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Replace custom LtiAuthenticationProvider with uk.ac.ox.ctl.lti13.Lti13Configurer to handle LTI 1.3 authentication.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: The old LtiAuthenticationProvider class is removed or incompatible with the new LTI library version; using the new Lti13Configurer is required to compile and run LTI authentication.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Inject LmsDefaultGrantedAuthoritiesMapper to configure authority mapping in the new LTI configurer.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: The new Lti13Configurer requires a GrantedAuthoritiesMapper to function correctly; without injecting and configuring it, LTI role mapping will fail at runtime.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Update import statements to reflect the new LTI library structure and remove obsolete imports.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: The old import edu.iu.uits.lms.lti.security.LtiAuthenticationProvider no longer exists or is not used; the new library requires uk.ac.ox.ctl.lti13.Lti13Configurer.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Refactor security configuration to use constants for path matching instead of hardcoded strings.
Remove explicit CSRF and frame options disabling in favor of default behavior or library-managed settings.
Add /favicon.ico to the list of ignored static resources in WebSecurity configuration.
Reorder security configuration classes to ensure correct precedence of security filters.
Add /api/** paths to the REST security configuration to permit all access to API endpoints.
Replace deprecated Tomcat internal file upload exception with the standard Apache Commons FileUpload exception.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: The class org.apache.tomcat.util.http.fileupload.FileUploadException is part of Tomcat's internal API and may not be available or stable in newer JDK/Tomcat versions; switching to the standard library ensures compatibility.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Update base controller class to use the new OIDC-aware controller for Java 17/LTI 1.3 compatibility.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: The old LtiAuthenticationTokenAwareController likely relies on deprecated LTI 1.1/1.2 security mechanisms or APIs that are incompatible with the new LTI 1.3 framework required for the migration.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Replace LtiAuthenticationToken with OidcAuthenticationToken to align with LTI 1.3 OIDC standards.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: LtiAuthenticationToken is specific to older LTI versions; OidcAuthenticationToken is required for the new LTI 1.3 integration in the upgraded framework.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Update token retrieval and usage to use OidcTokenUtils for extracting user and system identifiers.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: The old LtiAuthenticationToken API for getting principal and system ID is replaced by OidcTokenUtils methods in the new LTI 1.3 implementation.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Add new launch endpoint to handle OIDC token validation and roster synchronization on application launch.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: The new LTI 1.3 flow requires explicit handling of the OIDC token and roster updates at launch time, which was not present in the previous controller structure.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Inject new services required for roster management and user synchronization in the new launch flow.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: The new launch logic depends on CourseService and SystemUserService which were not previously injected or used in this controller.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Update method signatures to accept OidcAuthenticationToken instead of LtiAuthenticationToken.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: Methods interacting with authentication tokens must accept the new token type to compile and function correctly with the new LTI 1.3 framework.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Refactor error message handling to use previewWarnings instead of errors for skipped users.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: This is a UI/UX improvement to distinguish between fatal errors and warnings, not a compilation requirement.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Update string utility method call from arrayToCommaDelimitedString to arrayToDelimitedString.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.why_exists: arrayToCommaDelimitedString is deprecated in newer versions of Apache Commons Lang; arrayToDelimitedString is the recommended replacement.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Comment out the entire ViewemLtiController class to disable the LTI launch functionality.
Replace deprecated Swagger 2 annotations with OpenAPI 3 annotations for Java 17 compatibility.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/con.general_idea: Remove unused Swagger 2 class-level annotations.

src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/mod.general_idea: Replace the Spring Data REST annotation with a custom internal annotation to decouple from Spring Data REST or change resource exposure semantics.
src/main/java/edu/iu/uits/lms/viewem/mod.why_exists: The class will fail to compile because the import for org.springframework.data.rest.core.annotation.RestResource is removed, but the annotation usage (implied by the context of such migrations, though not explicitly shown in the snippet, the import swap is the key indicator) or the intent is to switch to a custom annotation. If the annotation was used on a field/method, removing the import without removing the usage causes a compile error. However, looking strictly at the diff, only imports changed. If the annotation was used, it would be in the body. The diff shows only imports. Let's look closer. The diff shows `import edu.iu.uits.lms.viewem.service.RestResource;` added and `import org.springframework.data.rest.core.annotation.RestResource;` removed. This implies the code uses `@RestResource`. If the code body isn't shown changing, it's likely the annotation usage remains but the type changes. This is a breaking change if the custom annotation doesn't exist or isn't on the classpath, but in the context of a migration commit, it's a deliberate API switch. Without the new import, the code using `@RestResource` would fail to resolve the symbol if the old one was removed. With the new import, it resolves to the new class. This is a breaking change in terms of dependency/behavior, but is it a 'migration' breaking change? It's a code change required for the new architecture. I will classify it as breaking because it changes the runtime behavior/dependency of the annotation.
